summary: Addresses greptile-apps[bot] review #8 feedback: applies the '|| exit_code=$?' guard pattern to four unguarded command substitution assignments under 'set -euo pipefail' in verify_keystone.sh (Tests 2, 4) and verify_venv_builder.sh (Tests 1, 3). Each location now captures the exit code without triggering set -e, and an assert_eq check for exit code 0 precedes the existing content assertions. A review pattern file was added to prevent recurrence.

tests_checklist.item: All command substitution assignments in verify_keystone.sh use || exit_code=$? guard
All command substitution assignments in verify_venv_builder.sh use || exit_code=$? guard
assert_eq exit code check added before content assertions in all four locations
Pattern matches sibling test functions that already used the guard

code_quality_checklist.item: Follows existing || exit_code=$? pattern from sibling test functions
No dead code or unnecessary changes
Changes are minimal and focused on the reported issue

architecture_checklist.item: Fix is consistent with the established test script structure

dry_yagni_checklist.item: No duplicated logic — reuses existing assert_eq helper

fail_fast_checklist.item: Exit code checked before content assertions — fails fast on docker run failure

defensive_checklist.item: exit_code initialized to 0, preventing uninitialized variable under set -u

suggested_improvements: verify_python_base.sh Tests 2 and 3 (lines 36, 46) still have unguarded command substitutions: id_output=$(docker run ...) and path_output=$(docker run ...). These should also use the || exit_code=$? pattern for consistency. This file was not part of the current diff, so it is noted as a follow-up.
next_steps: Apply the || exit_code=$? guard to verify_python_base.sh Tests 2 and 3 in a follow-up commit to achieve full consistency across all container verification scripts

detected_patterns.name: Exit code guard for command substitution under set -e
detected_patterns.applies_when: Writing bash test scripts with 'set -euo pipefail' that capture docker run output via command substitution
detected_patterns.description: Every command substitution assignment (var=$(cmd)) where the command can fail must use '|| exit_code=$?' to prevent set -e from aborting the script before assertions run. The local declaration initializes exit_code=0, and an assert_eq check for exit code 0 is placed before any content assertions. This prevents false passes where the test script exits silently instead of recording a FAIL.

examples.code:
local version exit_code=0
  version=$(docker run --rm "$IMAGE" keystone-manage --version 2>&1) || exit_code=$?

  assert_eq "keystone-manage --version exits 0" "0" "$exit_code"

local version exit_code=0
  version=$(docker run --rm "$IMAGE" uv --version 2>&1) || exit_code=$?

  assert_eq "uv --version exits 0" "0" "$exit_code"
